title: Package + serve-certify the 399.75 byte-exact split-KV as a draw-ready candidate
run_prefix: LOCAL analysis_only (no HF job / no run_request.json); evidence dir research/speed/byteexact_attn/serve_run + research/validity/byteexact399_operative_cert/flip_census
failure_logs: none (0 fatal tracebacks across all arms; 2 benign usage-string tracebacks per arm, standard)

public_evidence_used.byteexact_kernel_399_75: #496 wandb 42qroec1 (fixed-order split-KV, 399.75 local TPS, PPL 2.3767, 0/8 kernel flips)
public_evidence_used.surgical_357_rung: #488 wandb ko01dcyy / stark #494 package (357.6 byte-exact e2e, PPL 2.3767, 1 ULP flip 0 semantic)

deliverable_1_package.arming: sitecustomize.py env-gated import byteexact_splitkv_patch -> if ENABLED(_FIXED_TPS>0): _arm() (meta-path finder pre-vLLM, immediate re-jit post-vLLM); STOCK-wheel, no installed-file edit
deliverable_1_package.byte_faithful_to: fa2sw_precache_kenyan (only the byteexact lever + patch file differ; VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT NOT set -> fast Marlin matmul kept)

deliverable_2_harness_fix.advisor_proposed_fix: drive M=1-AR reference through cudagraph not eager
deliverable_2_harness_fix.advisor_fix_noop_reason: serve.py disable_speculation_for_reference_mode() only clears SPECULATIVE_CONFIG; there is no enforce_eager anywhere -> the M=1-AR ref ALREADY captures+replays a width-1 cudagraph, not eager. Confirmed by static read of serve.py + both server.logs.
deliverable_2_harness_fix.full_flag_invalid_reason: VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1 patches ONLY the matmul family (mm/addmm/matmul/linear/bmm/_log_softmax) + NCCL/cuBLAS/TF32 env; it NEVER touches the attention kernel. So full_flag's Gemma global full-attn stays the stock ADAPTIVE 3D split-KV = M-DEPENDENT on the very axis under test -> it is structurally incapable of reading ~1.0. Wrong control.
deliverable_2_harness_fix.actual_fix: bump --ref-decodes 2->3 so the M=1-AR reference's WARM (r1-r2) self-determinism is measurable; the 2-round default only ever measured cold(r0)-vs-warm(r1), conflating cudagraph-capture/cache warmup with non-determinism.

validated_on_byte_exact_by_construction_.interpretation: both the served candidate AND its M=1-AR reference read warm r1-r2 = 1.000000 (0/128 flipped seqs) once >=3 rounds exist. The cold r0-r1 = 0.621 is exactly the confound the 2-round harness mislabeled as ref non-determinism.

deliverable_2_harness_fix.free_running_greedy_reason: one bf16-ULP tie at the argmax flips greedy and the whole suffix cascades to ~0%; end-to-end rate (e.g. 0.3977 in the 128x512 run) ~ first-flip-position/length = warmup/session noise, does not rank by M-invariance.

part_2_margin_census_461_locus.methodology: #461 logit-margin locus: teacher-forced M=8 prompt_logprobs verify vs M=1 AR (NO greedy cascade), 127 prompts x ctx 224 x M=8 verify; same methodology surgical-357 was certified on
part_2_margin_census_461_locus.byteexact_vs_surgical_standard: byteexact has 0 SEMANTIC flips (quality-safe; meets that bar) but 5 ULP near-tie flips vs surgical-357's 1; it does NOT match surgical's census tightness.
part_2_margin_census_461_locus.flip_overlap: byteexact closes deployed's 1 ATTENTION-induced flip (mmlu_pro-00996c6808) but its different 3D attention output lands on 3 NEW matmul knife-edge ties (all gpqa_diamond); keeps deployed's 2 matmul-induced flips.

attention_byte_exactness_reconciliation_.microbench: raw attention-output bytes (torch.equal int16), M=8 verify row-i vs M=1 AR same abs position, synthetic Gemma global-attn shape
attention_byte_exactness_reconciliation_.conclusion: #496 is CONFIRMED, not refuted: the byteexact attention kernel is byte-exact (0/8, exact-zero error) at the census positions INCLUDING the partial-last-segment region the census exercises. The 5 census TOKEN flips are therefore NOT attention-induced -> they come from the un-taxed fast Marlin matmul (M-dependent; byteexact deliberately keeps it for speed, exactly as surgical's attn_only does). Proven by the chain: (1) microbench attention byte-exact 0/8; (2) attn_only (M-inv 2D attn + un-taxed matmul) still has 1 flip -> matmul contributes; (3) byteexact (byte-exact 3D attn + un-taxed matmul) has 5 -> flip count is set by how byteexact's particular 3D attention output lands on the M-dependent matmul's knife-edges.

deliverable_3_operative_cert.verdict: Attention byte-exactness PASS (#496 reproduced) + served self-determinism PASS (r1-r2=1.0) + 0 semantic flips (quality-safe). BUT end-to-end census is LOOSER than surgical-357 (5 ULP near-ties vs 1), because byteexact keeps the un-taxed Marlin matmul (the +42 TPS lever). To match surgical's 1-flip census tightness byteexact would ALSO need the ~48% matmul tax, defeating the purpose. Honest call: faster (+42 TPS) byte-exact-ATTENTION candidate, served-deterministic and quality-safe, but NOT census-tight to surgical's strict standard.

serve_log_signatures.byteexact_armed_line: [byteexact] fixed split-KV armed: tiles_per_segment=4 num_par_softmax_segments=64 (coverage 4096 keys)

pristine_restore_verified.serve_venv_triton_unified_attention: adaptive cdiv_fn intact (lines 302, 681); no baked literal
pristine_restore_verified.note: the package re-jits in-memory only (inspect+exec); it never edits installed-wheel files, so 0 markers by construction
